preamble: The transformation of the high front rounded vowel [y] (or the palatal glide [j]) into the glottal stop [ʔ] is a form of debuccalization and extreme lenition, typically documented in the reduction of back-velar segments or through complex glide-stop interactions.

examples.note: Historical reduction where high-articulation segments debuccalized to glottal stops in Chechen and related dialects.
While usually part of a merger, the interaction between glottalized segments and palatal glides highlights the articulatory relationship in rapid speech.

sources: Phonological research on Nakh and English glide reduction.
